# Bank POC Agentic AI — Developer Guide

> **A comprehensive banking proof-of-concept powered by CrewAI multi-agent orchestration, Django, and machine learning models for loan underwriting, AML compliance, FD advisory, and mortgage analytics across Indian and US markets.**

---

## Table of Contents

1. [What This Project Does](#what-this-project-does)
2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
3. [Environment Configuration](#environment-configuration)
4. [Project Structure](#project-structure)
5. [Core Features](#core-features)
6. [API Reference](#api-reference)
7. [Agent & Tool System](#agent--tool-system)
8. [Database Schema](#database-schema)
9. [Testing](#testing)
10. [Deployment](#deployment)
11. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)

---

## What This Project Does

This is a **proof-of-concept digital banking platform** that uses AI agents to automate banking workflows. It demonstrates:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Loan Processing** | End-to-end loan lifecycle — application, credit scoring (India/US models), KYC verification, AML compliance screening, provider search, and approval |
| **Smart Assistant** | Conversational AI that routes user queries to specialized agent crews |
| **FD / TD Advisory** | Fixed Deposit rate comparison, booking, maturity tracking, and renewal recommendations |
| **Mortgage Analytics** | US-specific mortgage analysis with ML-driven risk scoring |
| **AML Compliance** | Sanctions screening via Yente/OpenSanctions, PEP detection via Wikidata, adverse media search |
| **Admin Dashboard** | User management, transaction monitoring, email campaigns, database query UI, model CRUD |
| **User Portal** | Registration, login, loan/FD/transaction views, password reset |
| **RAG System** | Retrieval-Augmented Generation for policy document search and compliance-aware decisions |

### Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Backend | **Django 6.0.4**, Django Channels (WebSocket) |
| AI Orchestration | **CrewAI 1.9.1** — multi-agent task decomposition and execution |
| LLM | **OpenCode Premium** (deepseek-v4-flash) or NVIDIA NIM endpoints |
| Databases | **SQLite** (development), **Neo4j** (entity graph), **ChromaDB** (RAG vectors) |
| ML | **XGBoost** (US credit risk), **Logistic Regression** (Indian credit risk), **Scikit-learn**, **CatBoost**, **LightGBM** |
| Frontend | Django Templates, **Apache ECharts** visualizations, responsive CSS |
| Observability | **Langfuse** — LLM tracing, evaluation, cost tracking |
| External APIs | Serper.dev (web search), GNews/NewsAPI (news), Yente/OpenSanctions (screening), ipinfo.io (geolocation) |

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- Python 3.10+
- pip
- A virtual environment (recommended)

### Installation

```bash
# 1. Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/Ganeshkumar-1508/bank_poc_agentic_ai.git
cd bank_poc_agentic_ai

# 2. Create and activate a virtual environment
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate

# 3. Install dependencies
cd Poc
pip install -r requirements.txt

# 4. Set up environment variables (copy and fill in)
cp .env.example .env  # (or create .env manually — see Configuration section)

# 5. Run database migrations
python manage.py migrate

# 6. Seed initial data
python seed_admin_data.py
python seed_data.py

# 7. Start the development server
python manage.py runserver 0.0.0.0:8000
```

Visit **http://localhost:8000** in your browser.

### Quick Verification

```bash
# Check the home page loads
curl http://localhost:8000/

# Test an API endpoint
curl http://localhost:8000/api/user-region/

# Run the health check
python healthcheck.py
```

---

## Environment Configuration

Create a `.env` file in the project root (`Poc/.env`) with these variables:

```ini
# ---- REQUIRED: LLM Provider ----
# OpenCode Premium (recommended)
OPENCODE_API_KEY=your_opencode_api_key_here
OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1

# OR: NVIDIA NIM
# NVIDIA_API_KEY=your_nvidia_api_key_here

# ---- OPTIONAL: External APIs ----
SERPER_API_KEY=your_serper_api_key_here      # Web search for provider discovery
NEWSAPI_KEY=your_newsapi_key_here             # Financial news search
GNEWS_API_KEY=your_gnews_api_key_here         # Alternative news source

# ---- OPTIONAL: Email (Gmail SMTP) ----
SMTP_SERVER=smtp.gmail.com
SMTP_PORT=587
SMTP_USER=your_email@gmail.com
SMTP_PASSWORD=your_app_password

# ---- OPTIONAL: Observability ----
LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY=your_langfuse_secret
LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY=your_langfuse_public
LANGFUSE_HOST=http://localhost:3000

# ---- OPTIONAL: Neo4j (entity graph) ----
NEO4J_URI=bolt://localhost:7687
NEO4J_USER=neo4j
NEO4J_PASSWORD=your_neo4j_password

# ---- OPTIONAL: Site URL (for email links) ----
SITE_URL=http://localhost:8000
```

> **Note**: The app will run with defaults even without API keys, but LLM-powered features (CrewAI agents, Smart Assistant, credit risk scoring) require an LLM provider key.

## Core Features

### 1. Loan Processing Pipeline

The loan lifecycle is managed through status transitions:

```
DRAFT → SUBMITTED → UNDER_REVIEW → KYC_PENDING → COMPLIANCE_CHECK → APPROVED/REJECTED → DISBURSED
```

Each step can be handled manually (via API) or automatically via CrewAI agents:

- **Credit Risk Scoring**: ML models score applications — XGBoost for US (FICO-based), Logistic Regression for India (CIBIL-based)
- **KYC Verification**: Extract identity data from uploaded document images
- **AML Compliance**: Screen applicants against sanctions lists (Yente/OpenSanctions) and Wikidata (PEP status)
- **Provider Search**: Find optimal loan providers with interest rate comparison
- **CrewAI Decision**: Full end-to-end automated decision using `loan_creation_crew`

### 2. CrewAI Agent System

The app uses **12 specialized agent crews**, each solving a specific domain:

| Crew | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| **System Router** | Routes user queries to the correct specialized crew |
| **FD Analysis** | Analyzes fixed deposit products and rates |
| **FD Research** | Searches for current FD rates and news |
| **FD Database** | Stores and retrieves FD data from database |
| **FD Visualization** | Generates ECharts visualizations for FD comparisons |
| **FD Template** | Creates FD certificate templates |
| **Loan Credit Risk** | Scores loan applications using region-specific ML models |
| **Loan Creation** | End-to-end loan processing (KYC → AML → scoring → provider) |
| **AML Execution** | Runs AML compliance checks (sanctions, PEP, adverse media) |
| **Mortgage Analytics** | US mortgage risk analysis using Fannie Mae models |
| **TD/FD Creation** | Creates Term Deposit / Fixed Deposit records |
| **Loan Provider Search** | Searches for optimal loan providers |

### 3. Smart Assistant

A conversational AI interface that:
- Detects user intent from natural language

## Agent & Tool System

### How Agents Work

The application uses **CrewAI** to orchestrate AI agents. Each agent has:

- **A role** (e.g., "FD Research Analyst") — defines its expertise
- **A goal** (e.g., "Find and compare FD rates") — defines its objective  
- **Backstory** — provides context and personality
- **Tools** — 1-2 specific tools it can use (intentionally limited to reduce bloat)
- **LLM** — configured model (OpenCode deepseek-v4-flash or NVIDIA)

Agents are organized into **crews** — teams of agents that work together sequentially:

```
Crew → Agent 1 (Task A) → Agent 2 (Task B) → Agent 3 (Task C) → Output
```

Example **AML Compliance Crew** flow:

```
Router Agent → Sanctions Check Agent (Yente Tool) → PEP Check Agent (Wikidata Tool)
            → Adverse Media Agent (News Search) → Report Writer Agent → Final Report
```

### Available Tools

Each tool is a Python class or decorated function in `Poc/tools/`:

| Tool | Used By | Capability |
|------|---------|------------|
| `BankDatabaseTool` | FD/DB agents | Execute SQL queries against SQLite |
| `LoanDatabaseTool` | Loan agents | CRUD operations on loan records |
| `SafeNL2SQLTool` | DB agents | Convert natural language to SQL |
| `UniversalDepositCreationTool` | TD/FD agents | Create fixed deposit records |
| `Neo4jQueryTool` | AML agents | Query entity relationship graph |
| `GraphCypherQATool` | AML agents | NL → Cypher for Neo4j |
| `YenteEntitySearchTool` | AML agents | Sanctions list screening |
| `WikidataOSINTTool` | AML agents | PEP status and entity enrichment |
| `EmailSenderTool` | Various | Send emails via Gmail SMTP |
| `MarkdownPDFTool` | Report agents | Convert markdown to PDF |
| `EChartsBuilderTool` | Visualization | Generate ECharts JSON |
| `LoanUSCreditRiskScorerTool` | Credit agents | Score US loans (XGBoost) |
| `LoanIndianCreditRiskScorerTool` | Credit agents | Score Indian loans (LogReg) |
| `AMLRiskScorerTool` | AML agents | Calculate AML risk scores |
| `US_Mortgage_Analytics_Tool` | Mortgage agents | Fannie Mae mortgage models |
| `RAGPolicySearchTool` | Various | Search policy documents |
| `KYCVisionTool` | KYC agents | Extract data from ID images |
| `ProviderNewsAPISearchTool` | Research agents | Web search for news/rates |
| `search_news()` | Research agents | News search (DuckDuckGo fallback) |
| `news_api_tool` | Research agents | NewsAPI/GNews integration |
| `calculate_deposit` | FD agents | FD maturity calculation |

## Database Schema

> **Primary Database**: SQLite at `Poc/data/bank_poc.db`

### Key Models

**`LoanApplication`** — The core model for loan lifecycle tracking
- `application_id` (unique, auto-generated: `APP{timestamp}`)
- `status` (DRAFT → SUBMITTED → UNDER_REVIEW → KYC_PENDING → COMPLIANCE_CHECK → APPROVED/REJECTED → DISBURSED)
- `region` (IN/US)
- Applicant info (name, email, phone, DOB, PAN/SSN)
- Financial data (income, loan amount, term, purpose)
- Credit data (score, DTI ratio, existing loans)
- Collateral (value, type)
- Risk assessment (JSON), approval probability, risk grade
- KYC status & data (JSON)
- Compliance status & results (JSON)
- Provider selection (name, interest rate, EMI)
- Timestamps (created, updated, submitted, decided)
- Indexed on: application_id, (status, created_at), (region, status)

**`AccountApplication`** — New account applications
- Account type, customer name, contact details
- Region, status tracking

**`FixedDeposit`** — Fixed deposit records
- Customer name, amount, rate, tenure, maturity date
- Renewal tracking (auto-renewal flag, days until maturity)

**`AuditLog`** — Comprehensive audit trail
- Links to LoanApplication
- Action type, actor type/ID, details (JSON), IP address

**`CrewAIReasoningLog`** — Agent reasoning trace
- Full agent chain-of-thought for observability

**`UserSession`** — Session management
- Session ID, user data, region settings
- Crew output cache for performance

**`EmailCampaign`** — Email campaign management
- Subject, body template, recipient filter
- Status tracking (draft → sent → paused)

**`DatabaseQueryLog`** — Admin DB query audit trail
- SQL query text, user, timestamp

**`ComplianceRule`, `Configuration`** — Admin-configurable rules and settings

## Deployment

### Production Checklist

Before deploying to production:

1. **Security Hardening**
   - Generate a new Django `SECRET_KEY` (do NOT use the dev key)
   - Set `DEBUG = False`
   - Configure `ALLOWED_HOSTS` with your domain
   - Use HTTPS (configure `SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE = True`)
   - Rotate all API keys (OpenCode, Serper, NewsAPI)

2. **Database**
   - Migrate from SQLite to PostgreSQL for production
   - Update `DATABASES` in `settings.py`

3. **Static Files**
   ```bash
   python manage.py collectstatic
   ```
   Serve static files via nginx or CDN.

4. **Channels/WebSocket**
   - Replace `InMemoryChannelLayer` with Redis:
   ```python
   CHANNEL_LAYERS = {
       "default": {
           "BACKEND": "channels_redis.core.RedisChannelLayer",
           "CONFIG": {"hosts": [("localhost", 6379)]},
       },
   }
   ```

5. **ASGI Server**
   ```bash
   pip install daphne
   daphne -b 0.0.0.0 -p 8000 bank_poc_django.asgi:application
   ```

### Docker (Manual Setup)

Create a `Dockerfile` in the project root:

```dockerfile
FROM python:3.11-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY Poc/requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY Poc/ .
RUN python manage.py migrate
EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["daphne", "-b", "0.0.0.0", "-p", "8000", "bank_poc_django.asgi:application"]
```

### Nginx Configuration (Example)

```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name your-domain.com;

    location /static/ {
        alias /path/to/Poc/staticfiles/;
    }

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8000;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
    }
}
```

## Troubleshooting

### Common Issues

| Problem | Likely Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| `ModuleNotFoundError: crewai` | Dependencies not installed | `pip install -r requirements.txt` |
| Agents return no output | Missing LLM API key | Set `OPENCODE_API_KEY` in `.env` |
| `DatabaseError: no such table` | Migrations not run | `python manage.py migrate` |
| Admin login fails | No admin user seeded | `python seed_admin_data.py` |
| Smart Assistant not responding | CrewAI import error | Check `CREWAI_AVAILABLE` flag in logs |
| FD rates empty | No sample data | Run `python seed_data.py` |
| WebSocket disconnects | Channels layer not configured | Use Redis for production |
| PDF generation fails | Missing dependencies | `pip install markdown-pdf` |
| Email sending fails | SMTP not configured | Set `SMTP_USER` and `SMTP_PASSWORD` in `.env` |
| GEO IP detection always returns US | No ipinfo.io token | Rate-limited; detection works with dummy token |

### Checking Logs

```bash
# Django development server logs (console)
python manage.py runserver --verbosity 3

# Check specific loggers
export DJANGO_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG
python manage.py runserver
```

## What This Project Does

This is a **proof-of-concept digital banking platform** that uses AI agents to automate banking workflows. It demonstrates:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Loan Processing** | End-to-end loan lifecycle — application, credit scoring (India/US models), KYC verification, AML compliance screening, provider search, and approval |
| **Smart Assistant** | Conversational AI that routes user queries to specialized agent crews |
| **FD / TD Advisory** | Fixed Deposit rate comparison, booking, maturity tracking, and renewal recommendations |
| **Mortgage Analytics** | US-specific mortgage analysis with ML-driven risk scoring |
| **AML Compliance** | Sanctions screening via Yente/OpenSanctions, PEP detection via Wikidata, adverse media search |
| **Admin Dashboard** | User management, transaction monitoring, email campaigns, database query UI, model CRUD |
| **User Portal** | Registration, login, loan/FD/transaction views, password reset |
| **RAG System** | Retrieval-Augmented Generation for policy document search and compliance-aware decisions |

### Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Backend | **Django 6.0.4**, Django Channels (WebSocket) |
| AI Orchestration | **CrewAI 1.9.1** — multi-agent task decomposition and execution |
| LLM | **OpenCode Premium** (deepseek-v4-flash) or NVIDIA NIM endpoints |
| Databases | **SQLite** (development), **Neo4j** (entity graph), **ChromaDB** (RAG vectors) |
| ML | **XGBoost** (US credit risk), **Logistic Regression** (Indian credit risk), **Scikit-learn**, **CatBoost**, **LightGBM** |
| Frontend | Django Templates, **Apache ECharts** visualizations, responsive CSS |
| Observability | **Langfuse** — LLM tracing, evaluation, cost tracking |
| External APIs | Serper.dev (web search), GNews/NewsAPI (news), Yente/OpenSanctions (screening), ipinfo.io (geolocation) |

---

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- Python 3.10+
- pip
- A virtual environment (recommended)

### Installation

```bash
# 1. Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/Ganeshkumar-1508/bank_poc_agentic_ai.git
cd bank_poc_agentic_ai

# 2. Create and activate a virtual environment
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate

# 3. Install dependencies
cd Poc
pip install -r requirements.txt

# 4. Set up environment variables
# Create .env file with your API keys (see Configuration section)

# 5. Run database migrations
python manage.py migrate

# 6. Seed initial data
python seed_admin_data.py
python seed_data.py

# 7. Start the development server
python manage.py runserver 0.0.0.0:8000
```

Visit **http://localhost:8000** in your browser.

### Quick Verification

```bash
# Check the home page loads
curl http://localhost:8000/

# Test an API endpoint
curl http://localhost:8000/api/user-region/

# Run the health check
python healthcheck.py
```

---

## Environment Configuration

Create a `.env` file in the project root (`Poc/.env`) with these variables:

```ini
# ---- REQUIRED: LLM Provider ----
# OpenCode Premium (recommended)
OPENCODE_API_KEY=your_opencode_api_key_here
OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1

# OR: NVIDIA NIM
# NVIDIA_API_KEY=your_nvidia_api_key_here

# ---- OPTIONAL: External APIs ----
SERPER_API_KEY=your_serper_api_key_here      # Web search for provider discovery
NEWSAPI_KEY=your_newsapi_key_here             # Financial news search
GNEWS_API_KEY=your_gnews_api_key_here         # Alternative news source

# ---- OPTIONAL: Email (Gmail SMTP) ----
SMTP_SERVER=smtp.gmail.com
SMTP_PORT=587
SMTP_USER=your_email@gmail.com
SMTP_PASSWORD=your_app_password

# ---- OPTIONAL: Observability ----
LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY=your_langfuse_secret
LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY=your_langfuse_public
LANGFUSE_HOST=http://localhost:3000

# ---- OPTIONAL: Neo4j (entity graph) ----
NEO4J_URI=bolt://localhost:7687
NEO4J_USER=neo4j
NEO4J_PASSWORD=your_neo4j_password

# ---- OPTIONAL: Site URL (for email links) ----
SITE_URL=http://localhost:8000
```

> **Note**: The app will run with defaults even without API keys, but LLM-powered features (CrewAI agents, Smart Assistant, credit risk scoring) require an LLM provider key.
